Carved into one of the hardest types of rock on Earth, the facility sits inside Shirkuh granite that can withstand crushing pressures far beyond conventional construction materials. This material puts up the toughest barrier possible for even the most powerful American bunker-busting bomb - the GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrator.

How embarrassing for the USA

NEW President Donald Trump issued his latest threat to Iran on Saturday morning. He demanded they open the Strait of Hormuz or face increased consequences. Trump initially set the deadline to reopen the narrow waterway for late March. After Iran requested more time, he pushed it back to April 6 at 8pm ET. If this deadline lapses, Trump has said he is willing to resume bombing Iran's energy infrastructure, which is illegal under the Geneva Convention. Ever since the war in Iran broke out on February 28, the Strait has been a key point of contention because roughly 20 percent of the world's oil supply flows out from there.

  • 1 video

There really needs to be a lot more openness and understanding about it all and how our hormones change throughout our lives. EXCLUSIVE BY CHARLOTTE DALY: At 42, with the miles on the clock and the scars to prove it, Derek Chisora is still doing things his own way.  On a grey London afternoon inside camp, there is music thumping somewhere in the background, laughter echoing down the corridor, and Chisora - shirt damp with sweat, eyes alive - holding court like only he can ahead of his showdown with Deontay Wilder at the O2 this weekend. Adorable bunnies!

If this Easter bunny turned up to deliver your chocolate eggs, you'd be sure to know about it. Big-eared Junior weighs an impressive 21lb and, you may not be surprised to learn, is a Continental giant rabbit. He towers over his playmate Suzi, a Netherland dwarf rabbit who is just 1.5lb. In fact, she's so small that she loves to snuggle up under one of Junior's massive ears when they're on the sofa together.

The town house is located in Stow-on-the-Wold and dates back to the early nineteenth century.

Email

I absolutely adore owls. Have you seen the picture of how long their legs are behind all the feathers? We have owls galore where I live, hooting away all night. Helps me get off to sleep.

Stunning portraits show a dozen hand-reared owls who have been trained to take part in flying displays. The birds, ranging in age from one to 19 years old, were photographed at the Scottish Owl Centre in Bathgate. The youngest is Opia, an Ashy-faced owl, and the oldest is Zeus, a Eurasian eagle owl - who is also the largest bird at the centre.

  • share Here's looking at twit-twoo: UK flying display owls pose for camera
